explicit_text: My father settled in Los Angeles after coming to the United States, and my parents bought a small hotel in the Skid Row area. I was born in that hotel on Winston Street. explicit_text: The hotel my parents owned was located in Skid Row, Los Angeles, above a saloon and restaurant. It mostly served transient men, though a few permanent residents lived there. Growing up in that environment, I was exposed to a mix of people, including some decent folks and many who struggled with addiction or crime. My parents eventually sold the hotel and moved away, but we returned to Skid Row after selling our grocery store in 1938.
